description: Current promoted V5 browser/default checkpoint. BP + search225 solid-geometry junction run, step 12000 (= latest.pt). Warm-started from the promoted tess15-weighted checkpoint with no head reinit, preserving the close-pair radius-3 junction-offset recipe. Raises the training edge envelope max_edges 1200->5000 and adds 10,905 box-pleated + 6,451 SEARCH-22.5 rows via the v5-bp-search225 sampler under the v4-solid-geometry-replay profile (no dash/text/watermark obfuscators). Selected over steps 6000/11000 by a direct native-cp-v1 end-to-end bake-off (deterministic exact-solve replay, PyTorch-MPS): recovered 231 vs 214/212. Browser-onnx re-baseline (shipping numeric path) recovers 204, decisively above the 121 tess15 baseline; the win holds on the path the product ships.

checkpoint.note: latest.pt == step 12000 weights (val_loss null / skip_final_eval; latest.pt is the final training state). step_1000..step_12000 snapshots also retained under the same full/ dir.

inference.notes: junction_offset remains radius-normalized and must be decoded with radius-3 offset-vote clustering (product decode = junction-first-v1 + source-image line evidence; no vertex refiner). Browser ONNX export uses explicit per-image BatchNorm ops. Promoted for a large native-cp-v1 end-to-end recovery gain driven by higher junction recall (more CPs pass the topology gate); recall is the lever, not extras-filtering.

nativeCpV1EndToEnd.harness: tree-maker-rust replay_exact_solve_experiments (deterministic exact-solve, --threads 4) over gate-passing inputs captured by compare_exact_solve_benchmark --candidate-source junction-first-v1 --line-evidence-source source-image --parity-repair --skip-flat-folder; strict 2px, 25s solve; commit 6ba22bf1, built+run from same worktree (provenance guard)
nativeCpV1EndToEnd.note: Baseline reproduces PR #74's 121/17 exactly. Directional 16-core per-bucket (baseline->v5): easy 70->131, medium 49->87, hard 0->2. Held-out caveat: 3 finalists measured on native for selection (see report).

nativeCpV1EndToEndBrowserOnnx.note: Shipping numeric path (browser onnxruntime-web) via run-browser-dense-cache.mjs + deterministic replay. Confirms the win holds on the path the product actually runs.
nativeCpV1EndToEndBrowserOnnx.mpsToOnnxDelta: browser-onnx recovers ~12% fewer than PyTorch-MPS (204 vs 231), mostly from ~26 fewer CPs clearing the topology gate; still decisively above baseline
